item.title: Mbappe starts as France take on Morocco in first 2026 World Cup quarter final
item.description: Mbappe starts as France take on Morocco in first 2026 World Cup quarter final

item.title: Yato and Botia to start against England
item.description: Seasoned veterans Peceli Yato and Levani Botia have fought their way into the FIJI Water Flying Fijians line-up to take on England this Sunday in Liverpool. Also making their return to the starting lineup are Caleb Muntz, Mesake Doge, Tevita Ratuva, Lekima Tagitagivalu and Vuate Karawalevu on the wing.
item.content:encoded:
Seasoned veterans Peceli Yato and Levani Botia have fought their way into the FIJI Water Flying Fijians line-up to take on England this Sunday in Liverpool.

Also making their return to the starting lineup are Caleb Muntz, Mesake Doge, Tevita Ratuva, Lekima Tagitagivalu and Vuate Karawalevu on the wing.

In the front row are Eroni Mawi, captain Tevita Ikanivere, and Doge.

Ratuva and Isoa Nasilasila are at locks.

The loose forwards are Yato and Tagitagivalu while Demolition Man Botia is at No.8.

The halves pairing are Simione Kuruvoli and Muntz.

There is a new midfield combination of Josua Tuisova and Kalaveti Ravouvou after Semi Radradra was injured against Wales last week.

The back three are Jiuta Wainiqolo, Karawalevu and Salesi Rayasi.

On the bench for the forwards are Sam Matavesi, Livai Natave, Peni Ravai, Temo Mayanavanua, Elia Canakaivata, and Pita Gus Sowakula.

Frank Lomani and Isaiah Armstrong-Ravula are the backs reserves.

The Flying Fijians play England at 1.10am Sunday at Hill Dickinson Stadium, Liverpool.

In omorrow's matches, the All Blacks will play Italy at 5.05pm, Australia will host France at 7.40pm, while Japan takes on Ireland at 10.10pm.

On Sunday, South Africa will face Scotland at 3.10am, while Argentina will host Wales at 7.10am in San Juan.

item.title: Natabua High School girls ready for Western Division Raluve semi-final challenge
item.description: Vucago says from the Under-14s through to the Under-18s, Natabua has enjoyed an impressive campaign to compete for a place in the divisional finals. He says the achievement reflects the school's commitment to developing girls' rugby, with players, coaches and supporters working tirelessly throughout the season to reach this stage.
item.content:encoded:
Vucago says from the Under-14s through to the Under-18s, Natabua has enjoyed an impressive campaign to compete for a place in the divisional finals.

He says the achievement reflects the school's commitment to developing girls' rugby, with players, coaches and supporters working tirelessly throughout the season to reach this stage.

Vucago says as the stakes rise, as both their Raluve grade in the U:16 and U:18 are through,and now the focus has shifted to the knockout rounds, where every match will demand discipline, teamwork and resilience, and the players have spent the week sharpening their skills and fine-tuning their game plans, determined to continue the momentum that has carried them through the competition.

He says they are wary that other schools will want to knock them out and he is urging the girls to remain humble and focused, reminding them that previous results count for little once the semi-finals begin.

Vucago says the entire school community will be hoping their girls can rise to the occasion and keep alive the dream of bringing home Western Division Weet-Bix Raluve titles across multiple grades.

Looking at games this week in the Vodafone Deans division in the under 16 grade, Cuvu College takes on Natabua; in the U:17 grade, Sigatoka Methodist College takes on Natabua and in the U:18 game Natabua faces Tilak High School.

In other quarter-final fixtures in the U:18 grade Ratu Navula College takes on Ba Methodist High School.

In the Weet Bix Raluve catergory in the U:16 grade Sigatoka Methodist College plays Jasper Williams High School and in the U:18 grade Thomas Baker Memorial School takes on Natabua High School.

In other Raluve U:18 game Jasper William High School plays Navosa Central College.

All games will be played at the 4R Stadium in Ba.

item.title: France face Morocco in high-stakes World Cup quarter-final tomorrow
item.description: A place in the FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-finals is at stake when France and Morocco meet in a highly anticipated quarter-final clash tomorrow morning. France, making their 16th FIFA World Cup appearance, are chasing a third world title after triumphs in 1998 and 2018.
item.content:encoded:
A place in the FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-finals is at stake when France and Morocco meet in a highly anticipated quarter-final clash tomorrow morning.

France, making their 16th FIFA World Cup appearance, are chasing a third world title after triumphs in 1998 and 2018.

The 2018 champions and 2022 runners-up have once again looked like serious title contenders, with a squad packed with attacking quality and tournament experience.

France has scored 14 goals on their way to the last eight, with captain Kylian Mbappé leading the attack with seven goals at this year’s tournament.

Mbappé is also firmly in the race for the Golden Boot. Argentina captain Lionel Messi currently leads the scoring charts with eight goals, while Mbappé and Norway striker Erling Haaland are close behind with seven goals each.

Les Bleus will also look to Ousmane Dembélé and Michael Olise for attacking inspiration as they aim to move one step closer to another World Cup final.

Morocco, making their seventh World Cup appearance, will be out for revenge against France, who ended their historic 2022 campaign with a 2-0 victory in the semi-finals.

The Atlas Lions have once again shown their resilience, overcoming strong opponents and proving they can compete with the best teams in the world.

Morocco will rely on the creativity of Achraf Hakimi, Brahim Díaz and Ismael Saibari as they look to turn the tables on France and create another famous World Cup moment.

Kick-off for the clash between the two football powerhouses is scheduled for 8am tomorrow.

Meanwhile, in the other quarter-final matches, Spain will face Belgium at 7am on Saturday.

On Sunday, Norway will take on England at 9am, before Argentina face Switzerland at 1pm.

item.title: Vunimono High and Nakasi High to meet in Rewa Secondary Schools U15 and U17 finals
item.description: Vunimono High School has defeated Saraswati College 2-0 to book their place in the final of the Rewa Secondary Schools Football Association Under-17 competition. In the second semi-final, Nakasi High School also secured a 1-0 victory over Baulevu High School to book a spot in the final.
item.content:encoded:
Vunimono High School has defeated Saraswati College 2-0 to book their place in the final of the Rewa Secondary Schools Football Association Under-17 competition.

In the second semi-final, Nakasi High School also secured a 1-0 victory over Baulevu High School to book a spot in the final.

The two schools will now face each other for the Under-17 title, with the final scheduled to kick off at 12.30pm tomorrow.

Meanwhile, the stage is also set for an exciting Under-15 championship clash between Nakasi High and Vunimono High after both teams booked their places in the final earlier today.

Nakasi High defeated Bhawani Dayal Arya College 2-0, while Vunimono High edged Nasinu Muslim College 5-4 in a penalty shootout after the match ended in a goalless draw.

The Under-15 final will kick off at 9am tomorrow at Ratu Cakobau Park in Nausori.

item.title: Southern giants set the pace as Grammar, Marist and Nasinu dominate Vodafone Deans Southern Zone quarter-finals
item.description: The three schools have emerged as the dominant forces heading into the Southern Zone quarter-finals, with each school qualifying with four teams across the various grades. Their strong representation highlights the depth of talent, preparation and commitment within their rugby programmes as they look to secure places in the Southern Zone semi-finals and eventually earn qualification for the Vodafo
item.content:encoded:
The three schools have emerged as the dominant forces heading into the Southern Zone quarter-finals, with each school qualifying with four teams across the various grades.

Their strong representation highlights the depth of talent, preparation and commitment within their rugby programmes as they look to secure places in the Southern Zone semi-finals and eventually earn qualification for the Vodafone Deans national competition.

Suva Grammar School once again demonstrated its consistency across the grades, with disciplined performances and a balanced squad.

Their success reflects the school's continued investment in developing players from the junior grades through to the senior divisions and their participation at the Suva Rugby Union club level.

Marist enters the knockout stage with four teams carrying the hopes of a passionate Marist community eager to see another successful Deans campaign.

Nasinu Secondary School has proven to be one of the biggest success stories of this year's Southern Zone competition matching the achievements of Grammar and Marist with four quarter-finalists. Nasinu has shown remarkable depth and resilience, setting up what promises to be an exciting knockout stage.

With all three schools boasting four teams each in the quarter-finals, they have established themselves as the benchmark for the Southern Zone.

Other prominent schools that will be playing in the quarter-finals include Gospel High School, Lomary Secondary School, and Lami High School.

The quarter-finals will be played at Grammar and Veiuto Primary School grounds with the opening game between Grammar and Marist in the Under 14 boys quarter-final at 8.30am.

The Vodafone Deans Southern Zone Under-18 competition will head straight into the semi-final stage next week, with only five schools participating in the top grade this year.

In the other quarter-final matches, the Under-15 grade will see Grammar take on Assemblies of God High School.

In the Under-16 grade, SGS will face Lami High School, while in the Under-17 grade, Grammar will play Nasinu.

In the Under-19 grade, Namosi Secondary School will meet AOG at 2:50 pm at the Grammar ground.

item.title: Nakasi High and Vunimono High set for U15 final showdown
item.description: Nakasi High School has booked its place in the Under-15 final of the Rewa Secondary Schools Football Association Competition after defeating Bhawani Dayal Arya College 2-0 in an impressive semi-final performance. Nakasi High School took the lead in the 21st minute through Roko Cinavilakeba, before Arnav Mani doubled the advantage in the second half to seal the victory.
item.content:encoded:
Nakasi High School has booked its place in the Under-15 final of the Rewa Secondary Schools Football Association Competition after defeating Bhawani Dayal Arya College 2-0 in an impressive semi-final performance.

Nakasi High School took the lead in the 21st minute through Roko Cinavilakeba, before Arnav Mani doubled the advantage in the second half to seal the victory.

In the first semi-final, Vunimono High School edged Nasinu Muslim College in a dramatic contest.

After neither side could break the deadlock in regulation time or extra time, the match was decided by a penalty shootout.

Vunimono held their nerve to claim a thrilling 5-4 victory and book their place in the final.

The stage is now set for an exciting Under-15 championship clash between Nakasi High School and Vunimono High School.

The Under-15 final will kick off at 9am tomorrow at Ratu Cakobau Park in Nausori, with supporters encouraged to turn out in large numbers to cheer on the young footballers as they compete for the title.

item.title: Justin Bieber joins Madonna, Shakira and BTS for FIFA World Cup final half-time show

item.title: British wildcard Fery storms into Wimbledon semi finals
item.description: British wildcard Arthur Fery has continued his sensational Wimbledon run with a straight-sets victory over world number 10 Flavio

item.title: Fresh faces expected in All Blacks side to face Italy
item.description: The All Blacks are expected to make changes to their side for Saturday’s Test against Italy in the capital, with several players i

item.title: Egypt allege referee bias towards Argentina and Messi after dramatic World Cup exit
item.description: Egypt have alleged refereeing bias in favour of Argentina and Lionel Messi following their dramatic 3-2 loss in the FIFA World Cup

item.title: NSW take State of Origin series with 30-12 win over Queensland
item.description: New South Wales have produced a stunning 30-12 victory over Queensland at Suncorp Stadium to seal the 2026 State of Origin shield.

item.title: Vakatawa eyes Flying Fijians debut
item.description: Former France international Virimi Vakatawa, who could make his FIJI Water Flying Fijians debut against England this Sunday, says

item.title: David Tolu Young named Fiji flag bearer, Anahira McCutcheon baton carrier for Commonwealth Games
item.description: Fijian swimming sensation David Tolu Young will be the flag bearer, while Anahira McCutcheon has been selected as the baton bearer
